name: Imaginative
definition: the ability to form mental images or concepts of things that are not present or have not been experienced.

name: Abstract thinking
definition: This refers to an interest in theoretical and philosophical ideas and a curiosity about the workings of the world at a conceptual level.

name: self-reflection

name: Contemplative

name: Critical thinking

definition: The quality of being friendly, generous, and considerate towards others, often accompanied by a desire to help or support them.

name: passion for education
definition: A strong passion or desire to acquire knowledge and understanding, often characterized by curiosity, exploration, and a willingness to try new things.

name: broad-mindedness
definition: The quality of being receptive to new ideas, perspectives, and experiences, often characterized by a willingness to consider different viewpoints and to challenge one's own beliefs.

name: social awareness
definition: The ability to understand and navigate social situations and relationships, often characterized by empathy, communication skills, and the ability to read and interpret social cues.

name: transcendence
definition: A sense of connection to something greater than oneself, often characterized by a belief in a higher power, a sense of purpose or meaning, and a commitment to personal growth and development.

name: development mindset
definition: They have a growth mind-set and are committed to continually learning and improving in order to achieve their objectives.

name: drive
definition: They have a strong desire to succeed and achieve recognition for their accomplishments, and are willing to take on challenging tasks and projects in pursuit of their goals.

name: analytical thinking
definition: They are skilled at analysing complex information and data, using their analytical skills to identify opportunities and develop effective strategies for achieving their goals.

name: receptive to feedback
definition: They are receptive to feedback and criticism, using it as a tool to improve their performance and achieve their goals.

name: quality-oriented
definition: They have a strong focus on quality, ensuring that their work meets the highest standards and exceeds expectations.

name: genuineness
definition: They are authentic and true to themselves, staying true to their values and principles while pursuing their objectives.

name: tolerance for uncertainty
definition: They are comfortable with ambiguity and able to navigate uncertain or complex situations with ease.

name: concentration
definition: They tend to be focused and disciplined, directing their efforts toward the attainment of their goals.

name: results-oriented
definition: They are highly focused on achieving results, using their energy and drive to push towards their goals and measure their progress along the way.

name: inspiration
definition: They are highly motivated and driven to achieve their goals, using their need for achievement as a source of inspiration and energy.

name: tolerance
definition: They have patience and are willing to persevere over a longer period of time in pursuit of their goals.

name: self-starter
definition: They are proactive in pursuing their goals, taking initiative and actively seeking out opportunities to advance their objectives.

name: risk control
definition: They are able to manage risk effectively, identifying potential risks and taking steps to mitigate them in order to achieve their objectives.

name: self-drive
definition: They have a strong internal drive to achieve their goals, and are not dependent on external rewards or incentives to motivate them.

name: introspection
definition: They are introspective and self-reflective, using self-assessment and self-evaluation as tools for improving their performance and achieving their goals.

name: Action planning
definition: Individuals with an internal locus of control tend to be more effective at setting and achieving their goals, as they believe that they have the power to influence outcomes through their own efforts and actions.

name: Resilience
definition: Individuals with an internal locus of control tend to have greater mental toughness and resilience, as they believe that they have the power to overcome challenges through their own efforts and actions.

name: Receptiveness to feedback
definition: Individuals with an internal locus of control tend to be more open to receiving feedback and criticism, as they believe that they have the power to learn and grow from these experiences.

name: forward-thinking
definition: Individuals with an internal locus of control tend to have a greater sense of control over their lives and circumstances, as they believe that they have the power to influence outcomes through their own actions and efforts.

name: Drive to compete
definition: Risk-takers often possess a strong competitive drive, eager to succeed and outperform others.

name: Entrepreneurial spirit
definition: Individuals with a higher propensity for risk-taking tend to be decisive, able to make quick and confident decisions.

name: High pain threshold
definition: Risk-takers often possess a high level of adaptability, able to adjust to changing circumstances and environments.

name: relationship building
definition: Individuals with a higher propensity for risk-taking tend to be more impulsive, often acting quickly without much forethought.

name: Engaging in self-reflection
definition: Risk-takers tend to have a high level of self-confidence, believing in their own abilities and judgment.

name: Willing to learn
definition: Risk-takers tend to have a higher tolerance for uncertainty and are willing to take on risks even if the outcome is uncertain.

name: Nimbleness
definition: Individuals with a high level of innovativeness tend to be agile, able to respond quickly and effectively to changes in the environment or market.

name: Expressiveness
definition: Innovators often possess strong communication skills, able to articulate their ideas and persuade others to support their vision.

name: Judgment
definition: People with high self-efficacy tend to be effective decision-makers, able to make decisions quickly and confidently based on available information.

name: Enablement
definition: Innovators often possess a strong sense of empowerment, believing in their ability to make a positive difference in the world and feeling empowered to take action towards that end.

name: Holistic thinking
definition: Innovators often exhibit a strong sense of intellectual curiosity, seeking out new knowledge and understanding in order to fuel their innovative ideas.

name: Direction
definition: Innovators often possess strong intuition, able to trust their gut instincts and make decisions based on their inner sense of what will work.

name: Non-traditional thinking
definition: People with high self-efficacy tend to be highly motivated, driven by a strong sense of purpose and a desire to achieve their goals.

name: Grit
definition: People with high self-efficacy tend to be perseverant, willing to stick with tasks or projects even when progress is slow or setbacks occur.

name: Big picture thinking
definition: Innovators often possess strong systems thinking skills, able to understand and analyse complex systems and identify leverage points for change.

name: self reflector
definition: Individuals with high self-efficacy tend to engage in self-reflection, examining their beliefs and behaviours and making changes as needed to improve their performance.
